lesson.title: Lesson 36: A Chance in a Million

rights.notes: Migrated from legacy published content; migration does not establish redistribution rights.

segments.text: Lesson 36.

translation.text: 第36课。

segments.text: A Chance in a Million.

translation.text: 百万分之一的机会。

segments.text: Listen to the tape, then answer the question below.

translation.text: 听录音，然后回答以下问题。

segments.text: What was the chance in a million?

translation.text: 那个百万分之一的巧合是什么？

segments.text: We are less credulous than we used to be.

translation.text: 我们不像过去那么轻信了。

segments.text: In the 19th century, a novelist would bring his story to a conclusion by presenting his readers with a series of coincidences, most of them wildly improbable.

translation.text: 在19世纪，小说家常用一连串巧合来收尾，其中大多荒诞得难以置信。

segments.text: Readers happily accepted the fact that an obscure maidservant was really the hero's mother.

translation.text: 读者会欣然接受这样一个事实：一个默默无闻的女仆其实是主人公的母亲。

segments.text: A long-lost brother, who was presumed dead, was really alive all the time, and wickedly plotting to bring about the hero's downfall, and so on.

translation.text: 一个失散多年、被认为已经死亡的兄弟其实一直活着，而且正恶毒地密谋让主人公垮台，等等。

segments.text: Modern readers would find such naive solutions totally unacceptable.

translation.text: 现代读者会认为这种天真的结局完全无法接受。

segments.text: Yet, in real life, circumstances do sometimes conspire to bring about coincidences, which anyone but a 19th novelist would find incredible.

translation.text: 然而现实生活中，种种情形有时确实会共同造成巧合，除了19世纪的小说家，任何人都会觉得不可思议。

segments.text: When I was a boy, my grandfather told me how a German taxi driver, Franz Bussmann, found a brother who was thought to have been killed twenty years before.

translation.text: 我小时候，祖父给我讲过一个德国出租车司机弗朗茨·巴斯曼如何找到一个被认为20年前已经阵亡的兄弟。

segments.text: While on a walking tour with his wife, he stopped to talk to a workman.

translation.text: 他和妻子徒步旅行时，停下来同一名工人交谈。

segments.text: After they had gone on, Mrs.

translation.text: 他们继续前行后，巴斯曼太太

segments.text: Bussmann commented on the workman's close resemblance to her husband, and even suggested that he might be his brother.

translation.text: 说那名工人和丈夫长得极像，甚至提出他可能就是丈夫的兄弟。

segments.text: Franz poured scorn on the idea, pointing out that his brother had been killed in action during the war.

translation.text: 弗朗茨对这个想法嗤之以鼻，指出自己的兄弟已经在战争中阵亡。

segments.text: Though Mrs.

translation.text: 尽管巴斯曼太太

segments.text: Bussmann was fully acquainted with this story, she thought that there was a chance in a million that she might be right.

translation.text: 完全了解这段往事，她仍认为自己或许有百万分之一的可能猜对了。

segments.text: A few days later, she sent a boy to the workman to ask him if his name was Hans Bussmann.

translation.text: 几天后，她派一个男孩去问那名工人是不是叫汉斯·巴斯曼。

segments.text: Needless to say, the man's name was Hans Bussmann, and he really was Franz's long-lost brother.

translation.text: 不言而喻，那人果然叫汉斯·巴斯曼，确实是弗朗茨失散多年的兄弟。

segments.text: When the brothers were reunited, Hans explained how it was that he was still alive.

translation.text: 兄弟重逢后，汉斯解释了自己为何仍然活着。

segments.text: After having been wounded towards the end of the war, he had been sent to hospital and was separated from his unit.

translation.text: 战争快结束时，他负伤后被送进医院，因而同部队失散。

segments.text: The hospital had been bombed, and Hans had made his way back into western Germany on foot.

translation.text: 医院遭到轰炸，汉斯徒步回到了德国西部。

segments.text: Meanwhile, his unit was lost and all records of him had been destroyed.

translation.text: 与此同时，他所在的部队全军覆没，有关他的全部档案也被毁了。

segments.text: Hans returned to his family home, but the house had been bombed and no one in the neighborhood knew what had become of the inhabitants.

translation.text: 汉斯回到老家，却发现房子已被炸毁，邻居中没人知道屋里的人后来怎么样了。

segments.text: Assuming that his family had been killed during an air raid, Hans settled down in a village 50 miles away where he had remained ever since.

translation.text: 汉斯以为家人在空袭中遇难，便在50英里外的一个村庄定居下来，此后一直住在那里。
